_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for Module Exam data. Drives the per-module exam pages (pages/exams/exam-header-module-N.html) and the Module Exams section on pages/support/grading-info.html.

common.questionTypeDescription: multiple choice, true/false, and code tracing
common.questionPoolNote: Questions are drawn randomly from a question bank, so each student's exam is unique.
common.integrityStatement: This is an individual assessment. Do not share questions, answers, or screenshots with anyone. Do not use ChatGPT, Copilot, or other AI tools. Violations will result in a zero and referral to the Dean of Students.
common.readyPrompt: When you're ready, click the button below to begin. Good luck, developer!
common.canvasNote: [Canvas will display the “Take the Quiz” button here]

exams.subtitle: Chapters 2 & 3 • Variables • Input/Output • Decision Structures

chapters.topics: Variables, data types, input(), print(), f-strings, arithmetic operators

tips: Review your lab assignments — The exam tests the same concepts you practiced
Re-read the textbook examples — Especially code tracing exercises
Practice tracing code by hand — “What does this code print?”
Know your operators — // vs /, %, **, and vs or
Don’t second-guess yourself — Your first instinct is usually right

exams.subtitle: Chapters 4 & 5 • Loops • Functions • Modular Design

chapters.topics: while loops, for loops, range(), nested loops, input validation
Defining functions, parameters, return values, scope, random module

tips: Trace loops by hand — Track variable values through each iteration
Know your range() — range(5) vs range(1,5) vs range(0,10,2)
Understand accumulator patterns — Running totals and counters
Parameters vs arguments — Know the difference!
Scope matters — Local variables exist only inside their function

exams.subtitle: Chapters 6 & 7 • Files • Exceptions • Lists

chapters.topics: File reading and writing, with statements, try/except exception handling
Lists, indexing, slicing, list methods, iteration, mutability

tips: Know file modes — 'r' vs 'w' vs 'a' and what each does
Understand try/except — What happens when exceptions are caught?
List indexing starts at 0 — list[0] is the first element!
Slicing excludes the end — list[1:4] gives indices 1, 2, 3
Mutability matters — Lists can be modified; strings cannot

exams.subtitle: Chapters 8 & 9 • Strings • Dictionaries • Sets

chapters.topics: String methods, slicing, parsing, validation with .strip(), .lower(), .isdigit()

tips: Strings are immutable — Methods return new strings, don’t modify in place
Dictionary access — dict['key'] vs dict.get('key', default)
Keys must be unique — Duplicate keys overwrite previous values
Sets have no duplicates — {1, 2, 2, 3} becomes {1, 2, 3}
Know your set operations — Union (|), intersection (&), difference (-)

exams.subtitle: Chapter 10 • Classes • Objects • OOP Design

chapters.topics: Classes, __init__, instance attributes, methods, self, creating and using objects

tips: self is required — First parameter of every instance method
__init__ initializes — Runs automatically when creating an object
Class vs instance — Blueprint vs actual object
